source-discipline.reason: All three v10.14 changes are tied to actual git artifacts: rule #11(a) ordering is grounded in concrete six-commit failure record (SHAs listed); /670 numerator is grounded in the actual data_20260519060000 dedup migration; bootstrap grep change is grounded in actual section-header inventory across both prior and current session records.

bias_direction: v10.14 is authored at the close of the same session that surfaced the drifts it documents. The patches respond exactly to drifts observed THIS session — risk that the patches over-fit to the specific session's experience rather than addressing the broader drift class. The grep update in particular is calibrated to match the two most-recent session-record formats; an older session-record format (pre-architecture-split) would still not match. Decision to defer cross-historical compatibility to the future-archaeology session that needs it.
independent_reviewer_counterclaim: An outside reviewer would object that v10.14 is housekeeping bundled under a single bump and should arguably have been three separate bumps (one per drift, ordered by severity). Counter: the architecture-v2.3 PI-growth guidance discourages thrashing the PI version number for minor patches; v10.13 was itself a single-rule bump and the v10.12 amend pattern shows micro-bumps are acceptable within a version label. v10.14 stays under that budget by bundling three documentation-only patches under one minor-version bump.
